# frozen_string_literal: true

module Api
  module Org
    module V3
      # v3 api endpoints for ap recurring upsells
      class ActionPagesRecurringUpsellsController < BaseController
        before_action :set_upsell, only: %i[show update destroy]

        def index
          authorize %i[api org v3 action_pages_upsells_recurring]

          upsells = @account
                    .action_pages_recurring_upsells
                    .page(page)
                    .per(per_page)

          serialize_collection(upsells)
        end

        def show
          authorize [:api, :org, :v3, @upsell]

          serialize_obj(@upsell)
        end

        def create
          authorize %i[api org v3 action_pages_upsells_recurring]

          upsell = @account
                   .action_pages_recurring_upsells
                   .new(upsell_params)

          if upsell.save
            serialize_obj(upsell, status: :created)
          else
            serialize_errors_for(upsell)
          end
        end

        def update
          authorize [:api, :org, :v3, @upsell]

          if @upsell.update(upsell_params)
            serialize_obj(@upsell)
          else
            serialize_errors_for(@upsell)
          end
        end

      private

        def set_upsell
          @upsell = @account.action_pages_recurring_upsells.find(params[:id])
        end

        def upsell_params
          params.require(params_wrapper).permit(
            *WRAPPED_MODEL_PARAMS.map(&:underscore),
            details: {}
          )
        end

        WRAPPED_MODEL_PARAMS = %w[
          acceptActionQualifier
          acceptActionText
          askPercent
          body
          declineActionQualifier
          declineActionText
          impact
          internalName
          maximumAskAmount
          minimumAskAmount
          roundToNearestMultiple
        ].freeze

        wrap_parameters params_wrapper, include: WRAPPED_MODEL_PARAMS
      end
    end
  end
end

# popup recurring upsell
class ActionPagesUpsellsRecurring < ApplicationRecord
  MAX_BODY_LENGTH = 1100

  has_paper_trail versions: {
    class_name: "PaperTrail::ActionPagesUpsellsRecurringVersion"
  }

  belongs_to :account

  has_many :conversions,
           class_name: "ActionPagesUpsellsRecurringConversion",
           foreign_key: :recurring_upsell_id,
           inverse_of: :recurring_upsell,
           dependent: :destroy

  in_dollars :minimum_ask_amount
  in_dollars :maximum_ask_amount
  in_dollars :round_to_nearest_multiple

  validates :accept_action_qualifier, presence: true
  validates :accept_action_text, presence: true
  validates :ask_percent, numericality: {
    only_integer: true,
    greater_than: 0,
    less_than_or_equal_to: 100
  }
  validates :body, length: { maximum: MAX_BODY_LENGTH }
  validates :decline_action_qualifier, presence: true
  validates :decline_action_text, presence: true

  validates :maximum_ask_amount, numericality: { greater_than: 0 }
  validates :minimum_ask_amount, numericality: { greater_than: 0 }
  validates :round_to_nearest_multiple, numericality: {
                                          greater_than: 0
                                        },
                                        allow_blank: true
end

Validation for the `body` field comes from the model, not the controller. In `app/models/action_pages_upsells_recurring.rb:5` the constant `MAX_BODY_LENGTH = 1100` is defined, and the next block enforces it with `validates :body, length: { maximum: MAX_BODY_LENGTH }`. If you need a higher limit, raise the constant (for example to 1500) or remove the length validator entirely, then adjust the spec to match the new rule. There’s no database constraint in `db/structure.sql`—the column is a `text` field—so changing the validation is enough on the Rails side. After updating the limit, re-run the model tests and make sure any API docs or UI copy that mention the old limit are updated as well. Let me know if you’d like help changing the limit in code.

The schema (`CREATE TABLE public…`, `gen_random_uuid()`) shows we’re on PostgreSQL. The column itself is just `body text`, so the database doesn’t impose a hard limit—`text` in Postgres can store up to about 1 GB.

For product-facing fields like copy shown to donors, teams often keep the max somewhere between 500 and 2,000 characters to keep messaging readable and avoid layout issues. If you don’t have a UX constraint, you could bump the model constant to something like 2,000 characters; if you want practically “no limit”, you could drop the validation altogether. It just depends on how long you expect the copywriting to be and what the UI can comfortably display.

# frozen_string_literal: true

# Upgrade Workflow
class ActionPageUpgradeConfiguration < ApplicationRecord
  include PgSearch::Model
  include Anedot::ActionPageConfigurationUtils
  include Anedot::ActionPageCacheReset

  self.table_name = "upgrade_configurations"

  has_paper_trail versions: {
    class_name: "PaperTrail::ActionPageUpgradeConfigurationVersion"
  }

  pg_search_scope :search,
                  against: %i[name],
                  using: {
                    tsearch: {
                      dictionary: "english",
                      prefix: true
                    }
                  }

  scope :filter_by_q, -> (q) { search(q).with_pg_search_rank }
  scope :without_temps, -> { where.not("name like ?", "TEMP-%") }

  belongs_to :account
  belongs_to :action_pages_upsells_recurring, optional: true
  has_and_belongs_to_many :upgrades,
                          -> { order("upgrade_configurations_upgrades.created_at") },
                          class_name: "ActionPageUpgrade",
                          foreign_key: :upgrade_configuration_id,
                          association_foreign_key: :upgrade_id

  has_many :action_pages,
           foreign_key: :upgrade_configuration_id,
           dependent: :restrict_with_exception,
           inverse_of: :upgrade_configuration

  before_validation :generate_name_if_null
  before_validation :restrict_name, if: -> (p) { p.default }

  validates :name, presence: true, uniqueness: { scope: :account }
  validates :account_id,
            uniqueness: {
              scope: :default,
              conditions: -> { where(default: true) },
              message: "default already exists"
            }

  # This is a workaround so we can properly order `order_ids` by created_at
  def upgrade_ids=(ids)
    upgrades.clear unless upgrade_ids == ids
    super
  end

private

  def generate_name_if_null
    self.name ||= generate_config_name
  end

  def restrict_name
    self.name = "Default"
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

module Api
  module Org
    module V3
      # v3 api endpoints for upgrade configurations
      class ActionPageUpgradeConfigurationsController < BaseController
        before_action :set_config, only: %i[show update destroy]

        def index
          authorize %i[api org v3 action_page_upgrade_configuration]

          configuration = @account.action_page_upgrade_configurations
                                  .filter_by(filter_params)
                                  .without_temps
                                  .page(page)
                                  .per(per_page)

          serialize_collection(configuration)
        end

        def show
          authorize [:api, :org, :v3, @configuration]

          serialize_obj(@configuration)
        end

        def create
          authorize %i[api org v3 action_page_upgrade_configuration]

          configuration =
            @account.action_page_upgrade_configurations.new(configuration_params)

          if configuration.save
            serialize_obj(configuration, status: :created)
          else
            serialize_errors_for(configuration)
          end
        end

        def update
          authorize [:api, :org, :v3, @configuration]

          if @configuration.update(configuration_update_params)
            serialize_obj(@configuration)
          else
            serialize_errors_for(@configuration)
          end
        end

        def destroy
          authorize [:api, :org, :v3, @configuration]

          return head :method_not_allowed if @configuration.default?

          if @configuration.try_destroy
            head :no_content
          else
            serialize_errors_for(@configuration)
          end
        end

      private

        def filter_params
          params.slice(:q).permit!
        end

        def set_config
          @configuration = @account.action_page_upgrade_configurations.find(params[:id])
        end

        def configuration_update_params
          configuration_params.except(*%i[default])
        end

        def configuration_params
          params.require(params_wrapper).permit(
            *WRAPPED_MODEL_PARAMS.map(&:underscore),
            upgrade_ids: []
          )
        end

        WRAPPED_MODEL_PARAMS = %w[
          actionPagesUpsellsRecurringId
          default
          name
          upgradeIds
        ].freeze

        wrap_parameters params_wrapper, include: WRAPPED_MODEL_PARAMS
      end
    end
  end
end

module Api

      # v3 api for public action pages
      class ActionPagesController < BaseController
        skip_before_action :set_account, only: %i[crimson isp]
        skip_before_action :set_action_page, only: %i[crimson isp]
        after_action :cache_response

          serialize_obj(@action_page, view: :object)

        def crimson
          account = Account.find(params[:account_id])
          action_page = account.donation_action_pages.find_by!(crimson: true)

        end

        def isp
          account = Account.find(params[:account_id])
          action_page = account.donation_action_pages.find_by!(isp: true)

          serialize_obj(action_page, view: :object)
        end

      private

        def cache_key
          ["public", action_name, @action_page.cache_key].join("/")
        end
      end
    end
  end

payload.message:
Two places pull that `body` column today:

- `app/controllers/api/org/v3/action_pages_recurring_upsells_controller.rb:11` loads `@account.action_pages_recurring_upsells` and hands each record to `Api::Org::V3::ActionPagesRecurringUpsellSerializer`. That serializer (`app/serializers/api/org/v3/action_pages_recurring_upsell_serializer.rb:6`) exposes the `body` field verbatim, so the admin UI (and anything else using the org V3 API) receives the HTML string as-is.
- Whenever an action page is fetched, the serializer (`app/serializers/api/public/v3/action_page_serializer.rb:100`) includes the associated `recurring_upsell`, and the public serializer (`app/serializers/api/public/v3/action_pages_recurring_upsell_serializer.rb:6`) again exposes `body`. That’s what donors’ pages render, so any formatting change would need to be consumed there too.

Additionally, each conversion snapshot records the upsell’s attributes (including `body`) in JSON via `app/models/action_pages_upsells_recurring_conversion.rb:7`, so historical data also expects that field to be a string of HTML.

If you switch to another format (Markdown, structured JSON, etc.), you’d need to adjust those serializers (and anything downstream that renders the field) to transform the stored format into the HTML the clients currently expect, and consider how to migrate the saved snapshots.
